text_en: People prone to every fault they denounce are walking advertisements of the uselessness of their training. That kind of man can be of no more help to me as an instructor than a steersman who is seasick in a storm[...].

text_en: Zu den herrlichsten Schätzen die durch die Bemühungen anderer aus der Finsternis ans Licht gezogen sind werden wir geführt; kein Zeitalter ist uns verschlossen zu allen haben wir Zutritt [...] Die Zusammenfassung aller
text_it: Zu den herrlichsten Schätzen die durch die Bemühungen anderer aus der Finsternis ans Licht gezogen sind werden wir geführt; kein Zeitalter ist uns verschlossen zu allen haben wir Zutritt [...] Die Zusammenfassung aller

author: Seneca
text_en: me dulcis saturet quies; obscuro positus loco leni perfruar otio nullis nota Quiritibus aetas per tacitum fluat. sic cum transierint mei nullo cum strepitu dies. plebeius moriar senex. illi mors gravis incubat qui notus

author: Seneca
text_en: [I]ndulge the body just so far as suffices for good health. It needs to be treated somewhat strictly to prevent it from being disobedient to the spirit. Your food should appease your hunger your drink quench your thirst
text_it: [I]ndulge the body just so far as suffices for bene health. It needs to be treated somewhat strictly to prevent it from being disobedient to the spirit. Your food should appease your hunger your drink quench your thirst

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: To live is to owe life to die.
text_it: To live is to owe vita to die.

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: Living is the outside of dying.
text_it: Living is the outside of dying.

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: A long life is a curse if you have a short temper.
text_it: A long vita is a curse if you have a short temper.

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: The person you are mad at for being late could be late.
text_it: The person you are mad at for being late could be late.

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: The Use and Misuse of Children
text_en: Death devours not only those who have been cooked by old age; it also feasts on those who are half-cooked and even those who are raw.
text_it: Morte devours not only those who have been cooked by old age; it also feasts on those who are half-cooked and even those who are raw.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: Soon you will be dead and none of it will matter
text_it: Soon you will be dead and none of it will matter

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: Si len úbohá dušička nesúca mŕtvolu ako vravel Epiktetos.
text_it: Si len úbohá dušička nesúca mŕtvolu ako vravel Epiktetos.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: Najlepší spôsob obrany je nepodobať sa tým čo nám ubližujú.
text_it: Najlepší spôsob obrany je nepodobať sa tým čo nám ubližujú.

author: Epictetus
text_en: When you are alone you should call this tranquility and freedom and when you are with many you shouldn't call this a crowd or trouble or uneasiness but festival and company and contentedly accept it.
text_it: When you are alone you should call this tranquility and liberta and when you are with many you shouldn't call this a crowd or trouble or uneasiness but festival and company and contentedly accept it.

author: Seneca
text_en: The man who looks for the morrow without worrying over it knows a peaceful independence and a happiness beyond all others. Whoever has said I have lived' receives a windfall every day he gets up in the morning.
text_it: The man who looks for the morrow without worrying over it knows a peaceful independence and a felicita beyond all others. Whoever has said I have lived' receives a windfall every day he gets up in the morning.

author: Kevin L. Michel
source: The Power of the Present: A Stoic's Guide to Unyielding Focus
text_en: Imagine if you will a bird in flight. When it must overcome the gusts that hinder its path it beats its wings with unyielding force. Yet when it soars high in the sky it spreads its wings and rides the wind at peace in
text_it: Imagine if you will a bird in flight. When it must overcome the gusts that hinder its path it beats its wings with unyielding force. Yet when it soars high in the sky it spreads its wings and rides the wind at pace in

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: You can look unhappy but feel the opposite. Or vice versa.
text_it: You can look unhappy but feel the opposite. Or vice versa.

author: Mokokoma Mokhonoana
source: P for Pessimism: A Collection of Funny yet Profound Aphorisms
text_en: Not complaining is sometimes a show off of tolerance or patience.
text_it: Not complaining is sometimes a show off of tolerance or patience.

author: Seneca
text_en: Do not trust her seeming calm; in a moment the sea is moved to its depths.
text_it: Do not trust her seeming calma; in a moment the sea is moved to its depths.

author: Seneca
text_en: The place one's in though doesn't make any contribution to peace of mind: it's the spirit that makes everything agreeable to oneself.
text_it: The place one's in though doesn't make any contribution to pace of mente: it's the spirit that makes everything agreeable to oneself.

author: Seneca
source: Letters from a Stoic: Modern English Edition
text_en: Virtue alone affords everlasting and peace-giving joy; even if some obstacle arise it is but like an intervening cloud which floats beneath the sun but never prevails against it.
text_it: Virtu alone affords everlasting and pace-giving joy; even if some obstacle arise it is but like an intervening cloud which floats beneath the sun but never prevails against it.

author: Seneca
text_en: [T]he man who spends his time choosing one resort after another in a hunt for peace and quiet will in every place he visits find something to prevent him from relaxing. The story is told that someone complained to

author: Seneca
text_en: Resent a thing by all means if it represents an injustice decreed against yourself personally; but if this same constraint is binding on the lowest and the highest alike then make your peace again with destiny the

author: Seneca
text_en: Let us too overcome all things with our reward consisting not in any wreath or garland not in trumpet-calls for silence for the ceremonial proclamation of our name but in moral worth in strength of spirit in a peace
text_it: Let us too overcome all things with our reward consisting not in any wreath or garland not in trumpet-calls for silence for the ceremonial proclamation of our name but in moral worth in forza of spirit in a pace

author: Kevin L. Michel
source: The Power of the Present: A Stoic's Guide to Unyielding Focus
text_en: The world might rage around yet within the Stoic's mind a tranquil sea prevails. The Stoic remains anchored not carried away by the torrents of distraction but rather cultivating a steadfast presence in each fleeting

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it if you have to with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.
text_it: Never let the futuro disturb you. You will meet it if you have to with the same weapons of ragione which oggi arm you against the presente.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: If you work at that which is before you following right reason seriously vigorously calmly without allowing anything else to distract you but keeping your divine part pure as if you should be bound to give it back
text_it: If you work at that which is before you following right ragione seriously vigorously calmly without allowing anything else to distract you but keeping your divine part pure as if you should be bound to give it back

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: When you are disturbed by events and lose your serenity quickly return to yourself and don't stay upset longer than the experience lasts; for you'll have more mastery over your inner harmony by continually returning to

author: Marcus Aurelius
text_en: When force of circumstance upsets your equanimity lose no time in recovering your self-control and do not remain out of tune longer than you can help. Habitual recurrence to the harmony will increase your mastery of it.
text_it: When force of circumstance upsets your equanimity lose no tempo in recovering your se-controllo and do not remain out of tune longer than you can help. Habitual recurrence to the harmony will increase your mastery of it.
